intro: Gymnopetalum pectinatum is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique, pinnate leaves and showy, white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and research has identified several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases, such as arthritis and diabetes.
